meaning: n.宕囬珮,搴勪弗,姘旇川楂樺皻
examples: Huckleberry was filled with admiration of Tom's facility in writing, and the sublimity of his language.
Its true spirit lies in sublimity and divinity.
The soul which loves and suffers is in a state of sublimity.
Spurred on by the guidebooks of the day, well-heeled English gentlemen travellers rapturously recounted the sublimity of crashing surf and rocky outcrops in their journals.
